translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Paul, apostle of Jesus Christ byGod's will, and the brother Timotheus, to the assembly ofGod which is in Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Grace to you, and peace fromGod our Father, and [the] Lord Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed [be] theGod and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of compassions, andGod of all encouragement;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who encourages us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to encourage those who are in any tribulation whatever, through the encouragement with which we ourselves are encouraged ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because, even as the sufferings of the Christ abound towards us, so through the Christ does our encouragement also abound.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But whether we are in tribulation, [it is] for your encouragement and salvation, wrought in the endurance of the same sufferings which we also suffer,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (and our hope for you [is] sure;) or whether we are encouraged, [it is] for your encouragement and salvation: knowing that as ye are partakers of the sufferings, so also of the encouragement.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we do not wish you to be ignorant, brethren, as to our tribulation which happened [to us] in Asia, that we were excessively pressed beyond [our] power, so as to despair even of living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we ourselves had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not have our trust in ourselves, but inGod who raises the dead;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who has delivered us from so great a death, and does deliver; in whom we confide that he will also yet deliver;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ye also labouring together by supplication for us that the gift towards us, through means of many persons, may be the subject of the thanksgiving of many for us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For our boasting is this, the testimony of our conscience, that in simplicity and sincerity beforeGod, (not in fleshly wisdom but inGod's grace,) we have had our conversation in the world, and more abundantly towards you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we do not write other things to you but what ye well know and recognise; and I hope that ye will recognise to the end,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: even as also ye have recognised us in part, that we are your boast, even as ye [are] ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with this confidence I purposed to come to you previously, that ye might have a second favour;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to pass through to Macedonia by you, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and to be set forward by you to Judaea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore this purpose, did I then use lightness? Or what I purpose, do I purpose according to flesh, that there should be with me yea yea, and nay nay?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: NowGod [is] faithful, that our word to you is not yea and nay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Son ofGod, Jesus Christ, he who has been preached by us among you (by me and Silvanus and Timotheus), did not become yea and nay, but yea is in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whatever promises ofGod [there are], in him is the yea, and in him the amen, for glory toGod by us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he that establishes us with you in Christ, and has anointed us, [is]God,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who also has sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I callGod to witness upon my soul that to spare you I have not yet come to Corinth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not that we rule over your faith, but are fellow-workmen of your joy: for by faith ye stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have judged this with myself, not to come back to you in grief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if I grieve you, who also [is] it that gladdens me, if not he that is grieved through me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have written this very [letter] [to you], that coming I may not have grief from those from whom I ought to have joy; trusting in you all that my joy is [that] of you all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For out of much tribulation and distress of heart I wrote to you, with many tears; not that ye may be grieved, but that ye may know the love which I have very abundantly towards you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if any one has grieved, he has grieved, not me, but in part (that I may not overcharge [you]) all of you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sufficient to such a one [is] this rebuke which [has been inflicted] by the many;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that on the contrary ye should rather shew grace and encourage, lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with excessive grief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I exhort you to assure him of [your] love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For to this end also I have written, that I might know, by putting you to the test, if as to everything ye are obedient.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But to whom ye forgive anything, I also; for I also, what I have forgiven, if I have forgiven anything, [it is] for your sakes in [the] person of Christ;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that we might not have Satan get an advantage against us, for we are not ignorant of his thoughts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now when I came to Troas for the [publication of the] glad tidings of the Christ, a door also being opened to me in [the] Lord,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I had no rest in my spirit at not finding Titus my brother; but bidding them adieu, I came away to Macedonia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thanks [be] toGod, who always leads us in triumph in the Christ, and makes manifest the odour of his knowledge through us in every place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we are a sweet odour of Christ toGod, in the saved and in those that perish:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to the one an odour from death unto death, but to the others an odour from life unto life; and who [is] sufficient for these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we do not, as the many, make a trade of the word ofGod; but as of sincerity, but as ofGod, beforeGod, we speak in Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do we begin again to commend ourselves? or do we need, as some, commendatory letters to you, or [commendatory] from you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read of all men,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: being manifested to be Christ's epistle ministered by us, written, not with ink, but [the] Spirit of [the] livingGod; not on stone tables, but on fleshy tables of [the] heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And such confidence have we through the Christ towardsGod:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not that we are competent of ourselves to think anything as of ourselves, but our competency [is] ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who has also made us competent, [as] ministers of [the] new covenant; not of letter, but of spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit quickens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (But if the ministry of death, in letters, graven in stones, began with glory, so that the children of Israel could not fix their eyes on the face of Moses, on account of the glory of his face, [a glory] which is annulled;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: how shall not rather the ministry of the Spirit subsist in glory?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if the ministry of condemnation [be] glory, much rather the ministry of righteousness abounds in glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also that [which was] glorified is not glorified in this respect, on account of the surpassing glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if that annulled [was introduced] with glory, much rather that which abides [subsists] in glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore such hope, we use much boldness:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and not according as Moses put a veil on his own face, so that the children of Israel should not fix their eyes on the end of that annulled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But their thoughts have been darkened, for unto this day the same veil remains in reading the old covenant, unremoved, which in Christ is annulled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But unto this day, when Moses is read, the veil lies upon their heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when it shall turn to [the] Lord, the veil is taken away.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the Lord is the Spirit, but where the Spirit of [the] Lord [is, there is] liberty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we all, looking on the glory of the Lord, with unveiled face, are transformed according to the same image from glory to glory, even as by [the] Lord [the] Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore, having this ministry, as we have had mercy shewn us, we faint not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we have rejected the hidden things of shame, not walking in deceit, nor falsifying the word ofGod, but by manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every conscience of men beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if also our gospel is veiled, it is veiled in those that are lost;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in whom thegod of this world has blinded the thoughts of the unbelieving, so that the radiancy of the glad tidings of the glory of the Christ, who is [the] image ofGod, should not shine forth [for them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we do not preach ourselves, but Christ Jesus Lord, and ourselves your bondmen for Jesus' sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because [it is] theGod who spoke that out of darkness light should shine who has shone in our hearts for the shining forth of the knowledge of the glory ofGod in [the] face of [Jesus] Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the surpassingness of the power may be ofGod, and not from us:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: every way afflicted, but not straitened; seeing no apparent issue, but our way not entirely shut up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: persecuted, but not abandoned; cast down, but not destroyed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: always bearing about in the body the dying of Jesus, that the life also of Jesus may be manifested in our body;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for we who live are always delivered unto death on account of Jesus, that the life also of Jesus may be manifested in our mortal flesh;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that death works in us, but life in you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having the same spirit of faith, according to what is written, I have believed, therefore have I spoken; we also believe, therefore also we speak;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: knowing that he who has raised the Lord Jesus shall raise us also with Jesus, and shall present [us] with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all things [are] for your sakes, that the grace abounding through the many may cause thanksgiving to abound to the glory ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore we faint not; but if indeed our outward man is consumed, yet the inward is renewed day by day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For our momentary [and] light affliction works for us in surpassing measure an eternal weight of glory;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: while we look not at the things that are seen, but at the things that are not seen; for the things that are seen [are] for a time, but those that are not seen eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we know that if our earthly tabernacle house be destroyed, we have a building fromGod, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For indeed in this we groan, ardently desiring to have put on our house which [is] from heaven;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if indeed being also clothed we shall not be found naked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For indeed we who are in the tabernacle groan, being burdened; while yet we do not wish to be unclothed, but clothed, that [what is] mortal may be swallowed up by life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he that has wrought us for this very thing [is]God, who also has given to us the earnest of the Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore [we are] always confident, and know that while present in the body we are absent from the Lord,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for we walk by faith, not by sight;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: we are confident, I say, and pleased rather to be absent from the body and present with the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore also we are zealous, whether present or absent, to be agreeable to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we must all be manifested before the judgment-seat of the Christ, that each may receive the things [done] in the body, according to those he has done, whether [it be] good or evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord we persuade men, but have been manifested toGod, and I hope also that we have been manifested in your consciences.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [For] we do not again commend ourselves to you, but [we are] giving to you occasion of boast in our behalf, that ye may have [such] with those boasting in countenance, and not in heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whether we are beside ourselves, [it is] toGod; or are sober, [it is] for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the love of the Christ constrains us, having judged this: that one died for all, then all have died;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he died for all, that they who live should no longer live to themselves, but to him who died for them and has been raised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that we henceforth know no one according to flesh; but if even we have known Christ according to flesh, yet now we know [him thus] no longer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So if any one [be] in Christ, [there is] a new creation; the old things have passed away; behold all things have become new:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all things [are] of theGod who has reconciled us to himself by [Jesus] Christ, and given to us the ministry of that reconciliation:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: how thatGod was in Christ, reconciling the world to himself, not reckoning to them their offences; and putting in us the word of that reconciliation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We are ambassadors therefore for Christ,God as [it were] beseeching by us, we entreat for Christ, Be reconciled toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Him who knew not sin he has made sin for us, that we might becomeGod's righteousness in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [as] fellow-workmen, we also beseech that ye receive not the grace ofGod in vain:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for he says, I have listened to thee in an accepted time, and I have helped thee in a day of salvation: behold, now [is the] well-accepted time; behold, now [the] day of salvation:)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: giving no manner of offence in anything, that the ministry be not blamed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but in everything commending ourselves asGod's ministers, in much endurance, in afflictions, in necessities, in straits,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in stripes, in prisons, in riots, in labours, in watchings, in fastings,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in pureness, in knowledge, in longsuffering, in kindness, in [the] Holy Spirit, in love unfeigned,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in [the] word of truth, in [the] power ofGod; through the arms of righteousness on the right hand and left,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: through glory and dishonour, through evil report and good report: as deceivers, and true;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as unknown, and well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as disciplined, and not put to death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as grieved, but always rejoicing; as poor, but enriching many; as having nothing, and possessing all things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our mouth is opened to you, Corinthians, our heart is expanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are not straitened in us, but ye are straitened in your affections;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but for an answering recompense, (I speak as to children,) let your heart also expand itself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not diversely yoked with unbelievers; for what participation [is there] between righteousness and lawlessness? or what fellowship of light with darkness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and what consent of Christ with Beliar, or what part for a believer along with an unbeliever?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and what agreement ofGod's temple with idols? for ye are [the] livingGod's temple; according asGod has said, I will dwell among them, and walk among [them]; and I will be theirGod, and they shall be to me a people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore come out from the midst of them, and be separated, saith [the] Lord, and touch not [what is] unclean, and I will receive you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will be to you for a Father, and ye shall be to me for sons and daughters, saith [the] Lord Almighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore these promises, beloved, let us purify ourselves from every pollution of flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness inGod's fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Receive us: we have injured no one, we have ruined no one, we have made gain of no one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I do not speak for condemnation, for I have already said that ye are in our hearts, to die together, and live together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Great [is] my boldness towards you, great my exulting in respect of you; I am filled with encouragement; I overabound in joy under all our affliction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For indeed, when we came into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but [we were] afflicted in every way; without combats, within fears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he who encourages those that are [brought] low, [even]God, encouraged us by the coming of Titus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and not by his coming only, but also through the encouragement with which he was encouraged as to you; relating to us your ardent desire, your mourning, your zeal for me; so that I the more rejoiced.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if also I grieved you in the letter, I do not regret [it], if even I have regretted it; for I see that that letter, if even [it were] only for a time, grieved you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I rejoice, not that ye have been grieved, but that ye have been grieved to repentance; for ye have been grieved according toGod, that in nothing ye might be injured by us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For grief according toGod works repentance to salvation, never to be regretted; but the grief of the world works death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For, behold, this same thing, your being grieved according toGod, how much diligence it wrought in you, but [what] excusing [of yourselves], but [what] indignation, but [what] fear, but [what] ardent desire, but [what] zeal, but [what] vengeance: in every way ye have proved yourselves to be pure in the matter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So then, if also I wrote to you, [it was] not for the sake of him that injured, nor for the sake of him that was injured, but for the sake of our diligent zeal for you being manifested to you beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this reason we have been encouraged. And we the rather rejoiced in our encouragement more abundantly by reason of the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because if I boasted to him anything about you, I have not been put to shame; but as we have spoken to you all things in truth, so also our boasting to Titus has been [the] truth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his affections are more abundantly towards you, calling to mind the obedience of you all, how with fear and trembling ye received him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I rejoice that in everything I am confident as to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we make known to you, brethren, the grace ofGod bestowed in the assemblies of Macedonia;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty has abounded to the riches of their [free-hearted] liberality.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For according to [their] power, I bear witness, and beyond [their] power, [they were] willing of their own accord,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: begging of us with much entreaty [to give effect to] the grace and fellowship of the service which [was to be rendered] to the saints.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And not according as we hoped, but they gave themselves first to the Lord, and to us byGod's will.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that we begged Titus that, according as he had before begun, so he would also complete as to you this grace also;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but even as ye abound in every way, in faith, and word, and knowledge, and all diligence, and in love from you to us, that ye may abound in this grace also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I do not speak as commanding [it], but through the zeal of others, and proving the genuineness of your love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that for your sakes he, being rich, became poor, in order that ye by his poverty might be enriched.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I give [my] opinion in this, for this is profitable for you who began before, not only to do, but also to be willing, a year ago.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now also complete the doing of it; so that as [there was] the readiness to be willing, so also to complete out of what ye have.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if the readiness be there, [a man is] accepted according to what he may have, not according to what he has not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For [it is] not in order that there may be ease for others, and for you distress,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but [on the principle] of equality; in the present time your abundance for their lack, that their abundance may be for your lack, so that there should be equality.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According as it is written, He who [gathered] much had no excess, and he who [gathered] little was nothing short.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thanks [be] toGod, who gives the same diligent zeal for you in the heart of Titus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he received indeed the entreaty, but, being full of zeal, he went of his own accord to you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but we have sent with him the brother whose praise [is] in the glad tidings through all the assemblies;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and not only [so], but [is] also chosen by the assemblies as our fellow-traveller with this grace, ministered by us to the glory of the Lord himself, and [a witness of] our readiness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: avoiding this, that any one should blame us in this abundance [which is] administered by us;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for we provide for things honest, not only before [the] Lord, but also before men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we have sent with them our brother whom we have often proved to be of diligent zeal in many things, and now more diligently zealous through the great confidence [he has] as to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whether as regards Titus, [he is] my companion and fellow-labourer in your behalf; or our brethren, [they are] deputed messengers of assemblies, Christ's glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shew therefore to them, before the assemblies, the proof of your love, and of our boasting about you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For concerning the ministration which [is] for the saints, it is superfluous my writing to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I know your readiness, which I boast of as respects you to Macedonians, that Achaia is prepared since a year ago, and the zeal [reported] of you has stimulated the mass [of the brethren].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have sent the brethren, in order that our boasting about you may not be made void in this respect, in order that, as I have said, ye may be prepared;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest haply, if Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we, that we say not ye, may be put to shame in this confidence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I thought it necessary therefore to beg the brethren that they would come to you, and complete beforehand your fore-announced blessing, that this may be ready thus as blessing, and not as got out of you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this [is true], he that sows sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he that sows in [the spirit of] blessing shall reap also in blessing:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: each according as he is purposed in his heart; not grievingly, or of necessity; forGod loves a cheerful giver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod is able to make every gracious gift abound towards you, that, having in every way always all-sufficiency, ye may abound to every good work:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: according as it is written, He has scattered abroad, he has given to the poor, his righteousness remains for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he that supplies seed to the sower and bread for eating shall supply and make abundant your sowing, and increase the fruits of your righteousness:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: enriched in every way unto all free-hearted liberality, which works through us thanksgiving toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because the ministration of this service is not only filling up the measure of what is lacking to the saints, but also abounding by many thanksgivings toGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they glorifyingGod through the proof of this ministration, by reason of your subjection, by profession, to the glad tidings of the Christ, and your free-hearted liberality in communicating towards them and towards all;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in their supplication for you, full of ardent desire for you, on account of the exceeding grace ofGod [which is] upon you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thanks [be] toGod for his unspeakable free gift.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I myself, Paul, entreat you by the meekness and gentleness of the Christ, who, as to appearance, [when present] [am] mean among you, but absent am bold towards you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I beseech that present I may not be bold with the confidence with which I think to be daring towards some who think of us as walking according to flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For walking in flesh, we do not war according to flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the arms of our warfare [are] not fleshly, but powerful according toGod to [the] overthrow of strongholds;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: overthrowing reasonings and every high thing that lifts itself up against the knowledge ofGod, and leading captive every thought into the obedience of the Christ;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having in readiness to avenge all disobedience when your obedience shall have been fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye look at what concerns appearance? If any one has confidence in himself that he is of Christ, let him think this again in himself, that even as he [is] of Christ, so also [are] we.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For and if I should boast even somewhat more abundantly of our authority, which the Lord has given [to us] for building up and not for your overthrowing, I shall not be put to shame;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that I may not seem as if I was frightening you by letters:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because his letters, he says, [are] weighty and strong, but his presence in the body weak, and his speech naught.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let such a one think this, that such as we are in word by letters [when] absent, such also present in deed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we dare not class ourselves or compare ourselves with some who commend themselves; but these, measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves with themselves, are not intelligent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now we will not boast out of measure, but according to the measure of the rule which theGod of measure has apportioned to us, to reach to you also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we do not, as not reaching to you, overstretch ourselves, (for we have come to you also in the glad tidings of the Christ;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not boasting out of measure in other people's labours, but having hope, your faith increasing, to be enlarged amongst you, according to our rule, yet more abundantly

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to announce the glad tidings to that [which is] beyond you, not to be boasting in another's rule of things made ready to hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that boasts, let him boast in the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For not he that commends himself is approved, but whom the Lord commends.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Would that ye would bear with me [in] a little folly; but indeed bear with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I am jealous as to you with a jealousy [which is] ofGod; for I have espoused you unto one man, to present [you] a chaste virgin to Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I fear lest by any means, as the serpent deceived Eve by his craft, [so] your thoughts should be corrupted from simplicity as to the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if indeed he that comes preaches another Jesus, whom we have not preached, or ye get a different Spirit, which ye have not got, or a different glad tidings, which ye have not received, ye might well bear with [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I reckon that in nothing I am behind those who are in surpassing degree apostles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if [I am] a simple person in speech, yet not in knowledge, but in everything making [the truth] manifest in all things to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have I committed sin, abasing myself in order that ye might be exalted, because I gratuitously announced to you the glad tidings ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I spoiled other assemblies, receiving hire for ministry towards you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being present with you and lacking, I did not lazily burden any one, (for the brethren who came from Macedonia supplied what I lacked,) and in everything I kept myself from being a burden to you, and will keep myself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [The] truth of Christ is in me that this boasting shall not be stopped as to me in the regions of Achaia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why? because I do not love you?God knows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But what I do, I will also do, that I may cut off the opportunity of those wishing [for] an opportunity, that wherein they boast they may be found even as we.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For such [are] false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into apostles of Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [it is] not wonderful, for Satan himself transforms himself into an angel of light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is no great thing therefore if his ministers also transform themselves as ministers of righteousness; whose end shall be according to their works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again I say, Let not any one think me to be a fool; but if otherwise, receive me then even as a fool, that I also may boast myself some little.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What I speak I do not speak according to [the] Lord, but as in folly, in this confidence of boasting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since many boast according to flesh, I also will boast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye bear fools readily, being wise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye bear if any one bring you into bondage, if any one devour [you], if any one get [your money], if any one exalt himself, if any one beat you on the face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I speak as to dishonour, as though we had been weak; but wherein any one is daring, (I speak in folly,) I also am daring.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are they Hebrews? I also. Are they Israelites? I also. Are they seed of Abraham? I also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are they ministers of Christ? (I speak as being beside myself) I above measure [so]; in labours exceedingly abundant, in stripes to excess, in prisons exceedingly abundant, in deaths oft.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the Jews five times have I received forty [stripes], save one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thrice have I been scourged, once I have been stoned, three times I have suffered shipwreck, a night and day I passed in the deep:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in journeyings often, in perils of rivers, in perils of robbers, in perils from [my own] race, in perils from [the] nations, in perils in [the] city, in perils in [the] desert, in perils on [the] sea, in perils among false brethren;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in labour and toil, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Besides those things that are without, the crowd [of cares] pressing on me daily, the burden of all the assemblies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is stumbled, and I burn not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If it is needful to boast, I will boast in the things which concern my infirmity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod and Father of the Lord Jesus knows — he who is blessed for ever — that I do not lie.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In Damascus the ethnarch of Aretas the king kept the city of the Damascenes shut up, wishing to take me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and through a window in a basket I was let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Well, it is not of profit to me to boast, for I will come to visions and revelations of [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know a man in Christ, fourteen years ago, (whether in [the] body I know not, or out of the body I know not,God knows;) such [a one] caught up to [the] third heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I know such a man, (whether in [the] body or out of the body I know not,God knows;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that he was caught up into paradise, and heard unspeakable things said which it is not allowed to man to utter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of such [a one] I will boast, but of myself I will not boast, unless in my weaknesses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if I shall desire to boast, I shall not be a fool; for I will say [the] truth; but I forbear, lest any one should think as to me above what he sees me [to be], or whatever he may hear of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that I might not be exalted by the exceeding greatness of the revelations, there was given to me a thorn for the flesh, a messenger of Satan that he might buffet me, that I might not be exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this I thrice besought the Lord that it might depart from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to me, My grace suffices thee; for [my] power is perfected in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather boast in my weaknesses, that the power of the Christ may dwell upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I take pleasure in weaknesses, in insults, in necessities, in persecutions, in straits, for Christ: for when I am weak, then I am powerful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have become a fool; ye have compelled me; for I ought to have been commended by you; for I have been nothing behind those who were in surpassing degree apostles, if also I am nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The signs indeed of the apostle were wrought among you in all endurance, signs, and wonders, and works of power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in what is it that ye have been inferior to the other assemblies, unless that I myself have not been in laziness a charge upon you? Forgive me this injury.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, this third time I am ready to come to you, and I will not be in laziness a charge; for I do not seek yours, but you; for the children ought not to lay up for the parents, but the parents for the children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I shall most gladly spend and be utterly spent for your souls, if even in abundantly loving you I should be less loved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But be it so. I did not burden you, but being crafty I took you by guile.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did I make gain of you by any of those whom I have sent to you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I begged Titus, and sent the brother with [him]: did Titus at all make gain of you? have we not walked in the same spirit? [have we] not in the same steps?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have long been supposing that we excuse ourselves to you: we speak beforeGod in Christ; and all things, beloved, for your building up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I fear lest perhaps coming I find you not such as I wish, and that I be found by you such as ye do not wish: lest [there might be] strifes, jealousies, angers, contentions, evil speakings, whisperings, puffings up, disturbances;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest myGod should humble me as to you when I come again, and that I shall grieve over many of those who have sinned before, and have not repented as to the uncleanness and fornication and licentiousness which they have practised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This third time I am coming to you. In the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every matter be established.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have declared beforehand, and I say beforehand as present the second time, and now absent, to those that have sinned before, and to all the rest, that if I come again I will not spare.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since ye seek a proof of Christ speaking in me, (who is not weak towards you, but is powerful among you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for if indeed he has been crucified in weakness, yet he lives byGod's power; for indeed we are weak in him, but we shall live with him byGod's power towards you,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: examine your own selves if ye be in the faith; prove your own selves: do ye not recognise yourselves, that Jesus Christ is in you, unless indeed ye be reprobates?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now I hope that ye will know that we are not reprobates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we pray toGod that ye may do nothing evil; not that we may appear approved, but that ye may do what is right, and we be as reprobates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we rejoice when we may be weak and ye may be powerful. But this also we pray for, your perfecting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On this account I write these things being absent, that being present I may not use severity according to the authority which the Lord has given me for building up, and not for overthrowing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the rest, brethren, rejoice; be perfected; be encouraged; be of one mind; be at peace; and theGod of love and peace shall be with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Salute one another with a holy kiss.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the saints salute you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love ofGod, and the communion of the Holy Spirit, [be] with you all.
